Mod 4 -Main Lesson (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 5
Speaker 1: Hey guys, welcome back to Module Four. So by this point in the program you should
have crafted your Segmentation and Confirmation Sequence. And in doing so, we've
brought your prospect to a point where they feel verified and validated in their current
reality, which includes that moment of highest tension we spoke about, as well as their
aspirations. And we've given them a sense of renewed optimism, as well as the exclusive
empowerment to re-pursue it.
But while we have them excited, committed, and hopeful for that outcome, it's now
time for us to solidify why those outcomes are achievable. To breathe a new sense of
hope into the situation. And that's where the Pre-Launch really comes into play. It's
really time for us to hone in on this main goal. It's to reward your prospect's excitement
by showing them why those outcomes are achievable. And whereas the Segmentation
and Confirmation Sequence got them through the door and onto the journey, our major
job here is the construct the arguments that fully liberate them from past failures, and
give them not just hope and commitment but steadfast belief that the outcomes are
possible.
So here is what were are going to be coaching the the Pre-Launch sequence. So the first
thing is gonna be complete clarity over past failures, and why the old way wasn't
working for them. And in doing so, there's gonna be this relinquishment of self-blame
and judgment over those failures. That's one thing we really need to do, we need to
release them from all that blame, all that self-doubt, because those are gonna be things
that create resistance later on. So this is gonna be about forgiving that, relinquishing
that self-blame, so that they're gonna be empowered to act later.
And the biggest aspect of the Pre-Launch is going to be after we dissolve the
relinquishment and the self-blame. It's gonna be all about giving them a new set of
empowering and action oriented beliefs, and understandings, and attitudes. And this is
gonna span several categories. We need to get them on board with new beliefs about
the opportunity and outcome they're trying to achieve, and about the problem itself. It's
not just enough to say, it wasn't your fault. You need to say why. Why wasn't it their
fault? And that's something that most marketers fall short of. They'll say, it's not your
fault that XYZ. But without qualifying why it wasn't their fault, without being hyper
specific about why it wasn't their fault that understanding, that deeper attitude, that
deeper belief, just doesn't take root.
We're also gonna instill these new beliefs about the more empowering way to achieve
it. Which later on, your product or your program is gonna be uniquely qualified to serve.
As well as creating new beliefs about their abilities and capacities to use that new way,
and your ability to lead them through it. And finally, you do want to create those beliefs
and understandings about your competitors, as well as the commonly held conventions
of your industry.
And when you set up your arguments like this, when you really give this full spectrum
set of beliefs, what you're doing in turn is giving your prospect the gift of a more
favorable and treatable diagnosis. And we spoke about this a bit before when we were
looking at the levels of awareness, and tapping into symptomatically aware, and then
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 5 --

Mod 4 -Main Lesson (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 5
re-diagnosing it. So a more favorable diagnosis, but one they need to act on, is a very
powerful trigger, and one that we're really setting up here by layering in these beliefs.
Because urgency, urgency doesn't come from despair. Urgency comes from a small
window of hope, and the opportunity to sneak in before it closes.
Despair is essentially resignation. Well grounded hope, hope that is grounded in new
empowering beliefs and opportunity, is what creates the energy to fuel action. So it's
our job, that is our job. We need to ground our prospect's hope in the fertile soil of new,
empowering beliefs that give them the chance to turn inspiration into action, to activate
it. To activate it and really fuel them on that journey, and get them to seize that
opportunity while it's there. Because once you've given them that new diagnosis, there's
a responsibility. You've given them clarity, you've set those new beliefs, and it is up to
them at this point to act on it.
And essentially, what we're doing is we're amplifying our prospect's response-ability,
their ability to respond. And we've done that by bringing the clarity and closure over
past failures. By elucidating those shortcomings, by pointing out those commonly
accepted but non-serving methods and mindsets, which I call the sacred cows with sour
milk. These are the things they never think to second guess, because it just seems so
natural and so set in stone. But once you release them from it, once you slaughter those
sacred cows and give them the energy to pursue a new way, it becomes their response-
ability. It increases their ability to respond.
The other thing we're doing to increase that response-ability is we're indoctrinating
them into that new set of empowering beliefs, which creates that renewed optimism
and motivation. It's kind of seeing that light and just wanting to move towards is and
really use that, leverage these insights that you and not your competitors have. So that's
a very powerful thing that amplifies their response-ability.
And finally, it's the whole gift of that expert diagnosis, which earns you the exclusive
right to prescribe, and earns your prospect the ability to respond. The ability and the
response-ability to act on it. So those are the major points here.
And what were really tapping into int he Pre-Launch, once we've accomplished it, we've
set up this whole dynamic of it's not your fault, until it is. That is the enduring point that
we want to accomplish by the end of this sequence. Once you accurately and
successfully diagnose and prescribe in an indisputable, accepted way, your prospect can
no longer defer responsibility. So this is what we're going for. We've cleared them of
past mistakes, we've shown them a new path towards that result and outcome, and it is
now their responsibility to follow it. And that's something we have to do to keep them
accountable too, later on.
So we're gonna get into some theme plates and exploratory questions in just a few
minutes. But I just want to take an extra minute or so to talk about sacred cows with
sour milk, because this is one of the main things that I focus on when developing which
beliefs are gonna really have the most impact, which ones I need to be focusing on, and
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 5 --

Mod 4 -Main Lesson (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 3 of 5
which ones are gonna ... which ones our prospect is most ready and willing to hear and
accept. So that's really something that I want to take an extra second to describe.
But essentially, the sacred cows with sour milk are those conventional, commonly held,
yet oppressive beliefs that derail the progress of your market. So these are the beliefs
that may overwhelm, or frighten, or restrict them. They're oppressive, and they're non-
serving. Yet at the same time, these are the ones that are so ingrained and reinforced
that they don't even think to challenge them, even when they don't serve. And that's
why if you do, you're building a ton of goodwill and trust with your audience, because
you're giving them the gift of removing, or re-shifting these sacred cows that just aren't
serving.
So, your job as a conversion coach, is to deconstruct it and shift it in a stimulating a
logically satisfying way. Of course, it has to make sense. But the fact that it's in your
audience's best interest to be open to new, better, more empowering and encouraging
alternative, is what sets the stage of receptivity for you to do that. So this is where the
challenging aspect of coaching comes into play. You need to be willing to challenge your
market's assumptions, your market's sacred cows, and to do so with courage and
conviction.
So a few examples of sacred cows could be, I can't build an online business until I learn
how to use WordPress. That probably held me back more than anything, about eight
years ago, is I thought I had to learn code. I was going to Udemy and all sort of resources
to try to earn how to code because I thought that was a prerequisite for owning an
online business. And I probably struggled with that sacred cow for about two years, until
I can't remember who slaughtered it for me, but I'm sure I invested in their program.
Another one that I struggled with is, I can't play guitar until I learn how to read sheet
music, or cords, or tabs. And I think I invested in a few online courses that said you don't
have to do that. There were other methods of learning how to do it. So they slaughtered
that sacred cow for me, and earned a lot of trust and excitement. And more
importantly, I wanted to buy into that new belief, because the sacred cow wasn't
serving me, it was oppressive and holding my back from an outcome I really wanted to
have.
Another one in the online business world is, in order to have a six figure business I need
a massive list. I hear that one all the time. People think that their income in their online
business is gonna be totally dependent and correlated with the size of their list, instead
of just building a high converting funnel, something that can convert that five, eight, ten
percent. And then being able to afford to invest in the traffic, to put in a dollar in adds
and get five or ten dollars back. You don't need a massive list to do that.
Another one, in languages, I can't learn how to speak French because I'm terrible at
memorizing verbs. That might be a sacred cow that's holding back your prospects from
taking that leap with you. So these are things we want to deconstruct. We wat to help
them blast through these widely accepted, and non-serving beliefs, which in turn really
will liberate them. It frees them from hat perception, and it's really a gift.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Transcript by Rev.com
Page 4 of 5
When we talk about education and value, we're not just talking about how to
information. Sometimes being valuable and educational means educating people over
their past failures, giving value through encouraging them to get through those things
that have been insidiously holding them back. Slaughtering those sacred cows with
them, because you're an expert who can deconstruct it, and could lead them to a more
favorable place.
So education isn't just how to, it's why X happened, and why it doesn't have to happen
like that anymore. That is one of the more powerful, and not leveraged forms of online
education that I really encourage you to use in your Pre-Launch.
And this all culminates at the end of the Pre-Launch. It culminates with this belief that
the outcome can happen. So they're no longer just committed to it, they believe that
that outcome can happen. And there's even this growing sense of inevitability around it
in your presence. Even before you've shown them the opportunity, just in your presence
as an authority, someone who's elucidated all these sacred cows, and earned that trust,
and got them encouraged, there is this sense that in your presence these outcomes are
inevitable. And that's what we've set up.
So this is everything we're doing in the Pre-Launch on a conceptual level. It's really time
to take all of these concepts and ground it into the actually execution. So I want us to
take a little breather here. You could re-watch this if you feel like you need to steepen
these concepts a little more. But when you're ready, meet me in the workbook, meet
me in the theme plates, and you'll see exactly the questions we use to draw out and
extract the content for the Pre-Launch subsequence. And then the theme plates that
allow us to really kind of have the containers to make them active in an efficient way.
So that's it. One final note, if you're doing a webinar, for example the Pre-Launch is
actually gonna be the first 30 minutes of your webinar, before you introduce your offer,
which would be considered at the launch. So generally, in those first 30 minutes that's
where you're really creating new beliefs about the opportunity. You're maneuvering,
you're reframing, you're giving insights, and you're giving proper diagnosis. So this will
happen on the webinar itself, but it's still gonna be extremely beneficial for you to just
go through these practices and exercises and make sure that you're dialing in these
points when creating your webinar.
And then, if you're doing a three part video series/workshop/challenge, then you're
gonna be sending out Pre-Launch emails to point to that asset. So you're essentially
gonna be ... the main job of the emails are going to be to sell the asset, to draw curiosity
around the video, or the challenge, or whatever format you're using for that. So those
emails are gonna be shorter, more of a tease, and you're gonna see the theme plate
tease for those. The email itself won't have to do the heavy lifting, but they're still gonna
be vitally important to get people to consume the Pre-Launch content, whether it is in a
Facebook group, whether it's a video, a challenge, whatever you're doing. You're gonna
have to use those emails to encourage consumption.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

But, if you are gonna be following this course in full and doing the Minimum Viable
Launch where you're not recording any videos, you're not doing any challenges, you're
doing an email only funnel, then we're also gonna show you a theme plate that allows
you the container to create those shifts all within the email itself. So it is gonna be
longer than the emails you're gonna be using if you're just selling the asset like a video,
but there is a way to do it in email only. And you're gonna get that theme plate in a
minute.
So I'm gonna take a deep breath, get a drink. And I will meet you back in the workbook,
in the theme plates, so that we can wrap up the Pre-Launch and really get it done in a
powerful way. So that's it, I'll see you in the next video.
